<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakenotōge Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Yakenotōge Kofun | native_name =焼ノ峠古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yakenotoge Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yakenotōge Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yakenotōge Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|25|36|N|130|35|55|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Chikuzen, Fukuoka|Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|25|36|N|130|35|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yakenotōge Kofun'''|焼ノ峠古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] or [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]], located in the Shisojima neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/163931|title=焼ノ峠古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakenotōge Kofun is located is located on a ridge extending north from Shiroyama, a small, independent hill in the northwestern part of the Chikugo Plain. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" style ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} tumulus, which is extremely rare for Kyushu. It is orientated to the south-south east, and has a total length of approximately 40.5 meters. The posterior portion is 23.5 meters on each side, and approximately 4.5 meters in height.The anterior part is 17 meters long, 12 meters wide, and approximately two meters high, and the width at the waist is 8.5 meters. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat about two meters wide. The posterior portion is thought to have been constructed in two stages, with the second stage being a mound of earth on top of the first stage, which was carved out of the ground. The anterior portion was entirely carved out of the ground. A vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the center of the posterior portion; however, a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not been performed.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
An ancient-style double-rimmed jar and a wide-mouthed jar was found near the surrounding moat. Judging from these [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery shards, the tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period. The tumulus is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Tsuko Station|Tsuko Station]] on the [[:en:Nishitetsu|Nishitetsu]] [[:en:Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://chikuzen.ed.jp/archaeology/cultural09.htmlChikuzen Town Board of Education home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/11112 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}}
Yakenotōge Kofun

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Yakenotōge Kofun | native_name =焼ノ峠古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yakenotoge Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yakenotōge Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yakenotōge Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|25|36|N|130|35|55|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Chikuzen, Fukuoka|Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|25|36|N|130|35|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yakenotōge Kofun'''|焼ノ峠古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] or [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]], located in the Shisojima neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/163931|title=焼ノ峠古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakenotōge Kofun is located is located on a ridge extending north from Shiroyama, a small, independent hill in the northwestern part of the Chikugo Plain. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" style ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} tumulus, which is extremely rare for Kyushu. It is orientated to the south-south east, and has a total length of approximately 40.5 meters. The posterior portion is 23.5 meters on each side, and approximately 4.5 meters in height.The anterior part is 17 meters long, 12 meters wide, and approximately two meters high, and the width at the waist is 8.5 meters. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat about two meters wide. The posterior portion is thought to have been constructed in two stages, with the second stage being a mound of earth on top of the first stage, which was carved out of the ground. The anterior portion was entirely carved out of the ground. A vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the center of the posterior portion; however, a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not been performed.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
An ancient-style double-rimmed jar and a wide-mouthed jar was found near the surrounding moat. Judging from these [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery shards, the tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period. The tumulus is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Tsuko Station|Tsuko Station]] on the [[:en:Nishitetsu|Nishitetsu]] [[:en:Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://chikuzen.ed.jp/archaeology/cultural09.htmlChikuzen Town Board of Education home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/11112 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}}
Yakenotōge Kofun

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Yakenotōge Kofun | native_name =焼ノ峠古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yakenotoge Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yakenotōge Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yakenotōge Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|25|36|N|130|35|55|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Chikuzen, Fukuoka|Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|25|36|N|130|35|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yakenotōge Kofun'''|焼ノ峠古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] or [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]], located in the Shisojima neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/163931|title=焼ノ峠古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakenotōge Kofun is located is located on a ridge extending north from Shiroyama, a small, independent hill in the northwestern part of the Chikugo Plain. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" style ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} tumulus, which is extremely rare for Kyushu. It is orientated to the south-south east, and has a total length of approximately 40.5 meters. The posterior portion is 23.5 meters on each side, and approximately 4.5 meters in height.The anterior part is 17 meters long, 12 meters wide, and approximately two meters high, and the width at the waist is 8.5 meters. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat about two meters wide. The posterior portion is thought to have been constructed in two stages, with the second stage being a mound of earth on top of the first stage, which was carved out of the ground. The anterior portion was entirely carved out of the ground. A vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the center of the posterior portion; however, a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not been performed.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
An ancient-style double-rimmed jar and a wide-mouthed jar was found near the surrounding moat. Judging from these [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery shards, the tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period. The tumulus is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Tsuko Station|Tsuko Station]] on the [[:en:Nishitetsu|Nishitetsu]] [[:en:Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://chikuzen.ed.jp/archaeology/cultural09.htmlChikuzen Town Board of Education home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/11112 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}}
Yakenotōge Kofun

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakenotōge Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Yakenotōge Kofun | native_name =焼ノ峠古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yakenotoge Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yakenotōge Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yakenotōge Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|25|36|N|130|35|55|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Chikuzen, Fukuoka|Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|25|36|N|130|35|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yakenotōge Kofun'''|焼ノ峠古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] or [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]], located in the Shisojima neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/163931|title=焼ノ峠古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakenotōge Kofun is located is located on a ridge extending north from Shiroyama, a small, independent hill in the northwestern part of the Chikugo Plain. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" style ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} tumulus, which is extremely rare for Kyushu. It is orientated to the south-south east, and has a total length of approximately 40.5 meters. The posterior portion is 23.5 meters on each side, and approximately 4.5 meters in height.The anterior part is 17 meters long, 12 meters wide, and approximately two meters high, and the width at the waist is 8.5 meters. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat about two meters wide. The posterior portion is thought to have been constructed in two stages, with the second stage being a mound of earth on top of the first stage, which was carved out of the ground. The anterior portion was entirely carved out of the ground. A vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the center of the posterior portion; however, a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not been performed.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
An ancient-style double-rimmed jar and a wide-mouthed jar was found near the surrounding moat. Judging from these [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery shards, the tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period. The tumulus is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Tsuko Station|Tsuko Station]] on the [[:en:Nishitetsu|Nishitetsu]] [[:en:Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://chikuzen.ed.jp/archaeology/cultural09.htmlChikuzen Town Board of Education home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/11112 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}}
Yakenotōge Kofun
{{Kofun navbar}}

[[Category:History of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Chikuzen, Fukuoka]]
}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Yakenotōge Kofun | native_name =焼ノ峠古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yakenotoge Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yakenotōge Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yakenotōge Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|25|36|N|130|35|55|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Chikuzen, Fukuoka|Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|25|36|N|130|35|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yakenotōge Kofun'''|焼ノ峠古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] or [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]], located in the Shisojima neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/163931|title=焼ノ峠古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakenotōge Kofun is located is located on a ridge extending north from Shiroyama, a small, independent hill in the northwestern part of the Chikugo Plain. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" style ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} tumulus, which is extremely rare for Kyushu. It is orientated to the south-south east, and has a total length of approximately 40.5 meters. The posterior portion is 23.5 meters on each side, and approximately 4.5 meters in height.The anterior part is 17 meters long, 12 meters wide, and approximately two meters high, and the width at the waist is 8.5 meters. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat about two meters wide. The posterior portion is thought to have been constructed in two stages, with the second stage being a mound of earth on top of the first stage, which was carved out of the ground. The anterior portion was entirely carved out of the ground. A vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the center of the posterior portion; however, a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not been performed.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
An ancient-style double-rimmed jar and a wide-mouthed jar was found near the surrounding moat. Judging from these [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery shards, the tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period. The tumulus is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Tsuko Station|Tsuko Station]] on the [[:en:Nishitetsu|Nishitetsu]] [[:en:Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://chikuzen.ed.jp/archaeology/cultural09.htmlChikuzen Town Board of Education home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/11112 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Yakenotōge Kofun | native_name =焼ノ峠古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yakenotoge Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yakenotōge Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yakenotōge Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|25|36|N|130|35|55|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Chikuzen, Fukuoka|Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|25|36|N|130|35|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yakenotōge Kofun'''|焼ノ峠古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] or [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]], located in the Shisojima neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/163931|title=焼ノ峠古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakenotōge Kofun is located is located on a ridge extending north from Shiroyama, a small, independent hill in the northwestern part of the Chikugo Plain. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" style ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} tumulus, which is extremely rare for Kyushu. It is orientated to the south-south east, and has a total length of approximately 40.5 meters. The posterior portion is 23.5 meters on each side, and approximately 4.5 meters in height.The anterior part is 17 meters long, 12 meters wide, and approximately two meters high, and the width at the waist is 8.5 meters. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat about two meters wide. The posterior portion is thought to have been constructed in two stages, with the second stage being a mound of earth on top of the first stage, which was carved out of the ground. The anterior portion was entirely carved out of the ground. A vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the center of the posterior portion; however, a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not been performed.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
An ancient-style double-rimmed jar and a wide-mouthed jar was found near the surrounding moat. Judging from these [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery shards, the tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period. The tumulus is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Tsuko Station|Tsuko Station]] on the [[:en:Nishitetsu|Nishitetsu]] [[:en:Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://chikuzen.ed.jp/archaeology/cultural09.htmlChikuzen Town Board of Education home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/11112 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Yakenotōge Kofun | native_name =焼ノ峠古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yakenotoge Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yakenotōge Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yakenotōge Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|25|36|N|130|35|55|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Chikuzen, Fukuoka|Chikuzen, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|25|36|N|130|35|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yakenotōge Kofun'''|焼ノ峠古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] or [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]], located in the Shisojima neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Chikuzen, Fukuoka| Chikuzen]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/163931|title=焼ノ峠古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakenotōge Kofun is located is located on a ridge extending north from Shiroyama, a small, independent hill in the northwestern part of the Chikugo Plain. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" style ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} tumulus, which is extremely rare for Kyushu. It is orientated to the south-south east, and has a total length of approximately 40.5 meters. The posterior portion is 23.5 meters on each side, and approximately 4.5 meters in height.The anterior part is 17 meters long, 12 meters wide, and approximately two meters high, and the width at the waist is 8.5 meters. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat about two meters wide. The posterior portion is thought to have been constructed in two stages, with the second stage being a mound of earth on top of the first stage, which was carved out of the ground. The anterior portion was entirely carved out of the ground. A vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the center of the posterior portion; however, a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not been performed.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
An ancient-style double-rimmed jar and a wide-mouthed jar was found near the surrounding moat. Judging from these [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery shards, the tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period. The tumulus is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Tsuko Station|Tsuko Station]] on the [[:en:Nishitetsu|Nishitetsu]] [[:en:Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://chikuzen.ed.jp/archaeology/cultural09.htmlChikuzen Town Board of Education home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/11112 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakenotōge Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakenotōge Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakenotōge Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Yakenotōge Kofun | native_name =焼ノ峠古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yakenotoge Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yakenotōge Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yakenotōge Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|25|36|N|130|35|55|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Chikuzen, Fukuoka|en|Chikuzen, Fukuoka|WD=Q1072679|lt=Chikuzen, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|25|36|N|130|35|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yakenotōge Kofun'''|焼ノ峠古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] or {{ill|Tumulus|en|Tumulus|WD=Q34023|lt=Tumulus}}, located in the Shisojima neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Chikuzen, Fukuoka|en|Chikuzen, Fukuoka|WD=Q1072679|lt= Chikuzen}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/163931|title=焼ノ峠古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakenotōge Kofun is located is located on a ridge extending north from Shiroyama, a small, independent hill in the northwestern part of the Chikugo Plain. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" style ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} tumulus, which is extremely rare for Kyushu. It is orientated to the south-south east, and has a total length of approximately 40.5 meters. The posterior portion is 23.5 meters on each side, and approximately 4.5 meters in height.The anterior part is 17 meters long, 12 meters wide, and approximately two meters high, and the width at the waist is 8.5 meters. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat about two meters wide. The posterior portion is thought to have been constructed in two stages, with the second stage being a mound of earth on top of the first stage, which was carved out of the ground. The anterior portion was entirely carved out of the ground. A vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the center of the posterior portion; however, a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not been performed.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
An ancient-style double-rimmed jar and a wide-mouthed jar was found near the surrounding moat. Judging from these {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} pottery shards, the tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period. The tumulus is approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Tsuko Station|en|Tsuko Station|WD=Q11555964|lt=Tsuko Station}} on the [[:en:Nishitetsu|Nishitetsu]] [[:en:Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakenotōge Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakenotōge Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakenotōge Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Yakenotōge Kofun | native_name =焼ノ峠古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yakenotoge Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yakenotōge Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yakenotōge Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|25|36|N|130|35|55|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Chikuzen, Fukuoka|en|Chikuzen, Fukuoka|WD=Q1072679|lt=Chikuzen, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|25|36|N|130|35|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yakenotōge Kofun'''|焼ノ峠古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] or {{ill|Tumulus|en|Tumulus|WD=Q34023|lt=Tumulus}}, located in the Shisojima neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Chikuzen, Fukuoka|en|Chikuzen, Fukuoka|WD=Q1072679|lt= Chikuzen}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/163931|title=焼ノ峠古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakenotōge Kofun is located is located on a ridge extending north from Shiroyama, a small, independent hill in the northwestern part of the Chikugo Plain. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" style ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} tumulus, which is extremely rare for Kyushu. It is orientated to the south-south east, and has a total length of approximately 40.5 meters. The posterior portion is 23.5 meters on each side, and approximately 4.5 meters in height.The anterior part is 17 meters long, 12 meters wide, and approximately two meters high, and the width at the waist is 8.5 meters. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat about two meters wide. The posterior portion is thought to have been constructed in two stages, with the second stage being a mound of earth on top of the first stage, which was carved out of the ground. The anterior portion was entirely carved out of the ground. A vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the center of the posterior portion; however, a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not been performed.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
An ancient-style double-rimmed jar and a wide-mouthed jar was found near the surrounding moat. Judging from these {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} pottery shards, the tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period. The tumulus is approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Tsuko Station|en|Tsuko Station|WD=Q11555964|lt=Tsuko Station}} on the [[:en:Nishitetsu|Nishitetsu]] [[:en:Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://chikuzen.ed.jp/archaeology/cultural09.htmlChikuzen Town Board of Education home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/11112 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}}

==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakenotōge Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakenotōge Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakenotōge Kofun]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Yakenotōge Kofun | native_name =焼ノ峠古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yakenotoge Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yakenotōge Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yakenotōge Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|25|36|N|130|35|55|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Chikuzen, Fukuoka|en|Chikuzen, Fukuoka|WD=Q1072679|lt=Chikuzen, Fukuoka|1=Chikuzen, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|25|36|N|130|35|55|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yakenotōge Kofun'''|焼ノ峠古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] or {{ill|Tumulus|en|Tumulus|WD=Q34023|lt=Tumulus|1=Tumulus}}, located in the Shisojima neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Chikuzen, Fukuoka|en|Chikuzen, Fukuoka|WD=Q1072679|lt= Chikuzen|1=Chikuzen, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/163931|title=焼ノ峠古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yakenotōge Kofun is located is located on a ridge extending north from Shiroyama, a small, independent hill in the northwestern part of the Chikugo Plain. It is a "two conjoined rectangles" style ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} tumulus, which is extremely rare for Kyushu. It is orientated to the south-south east, and has a total length of approximately 40.5 meters. The posterior portion is 23.5 meters on each side, and approximately 4.5 meters in height.The anterior part is 17 meters long, 12 meters wide, and approximately two meters high, and the width at the waist is 8.5 meters. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat about two meters wide. The posterior portion is thought to have been constructed in two stages, with the second stage being a mound of earth on top of the first stage, which was carved out of the ground. The anterior portion was entirely carved out of the ground. A vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was confirmed in the center of the posterior portion; however, a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not been performed.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
An ancient-style double-rimmed jar and a wide-mouthed jar was found near the surrounding moat. Judging from these {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery shards, the tumulus is believed to date to the early Kofun period. The tumulus is approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Tsuko Station|en|Tsuko Station|WD=Q11555964|lt=Tsuko Station|1=Tsuko Station}} on the [[:en:Nishitetsu|Nishitetsu]] [[:en:Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Ōmuta Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}
